Entity_Description: Richmond is a municipality of 209,937 residents (2021 Census) in Metro Vancouver, British Columbia, occupying Lulu Island and Sea Island in the Fraser River delta, about 13 km south of Vancouver. Incorporated in 1879, it is home to Vancouver International Airport (YVR) and the historic Steveston fishing village, once the centre of BC's salmon-canning industry and site of the Gulf of Georgia Cannery National Historic Site. Most of the low-lying city sits roughly one metre above sea level behind a network of dikes, with building stock ranging from City Centre high-rises along the Canada Line to postwar ranchers and active farmland in the east.

Short_Description: Richmond is a city built on a river delta — Lulu Island sits barely a metre above sea level, protected by dikes, with Vancouver International Airport humming on Sea Island next door. That geography shapes everything here, from the high water table under Steveston's heritage streets to the condo towers rising along No. 3 Road and the Canada Line. Whether you own a postwar rancher in Seafair, a Brighouse high-rise unit, or acreage out toward Hamilton, you get work matched to the ground it stands on. {CTA_PRIMARY} — call {PHONE}.
Long_Description:
Richmond exists because the Fraser River built it, one load of silt at a time. The city occupies Lulu Island and Sea Island in the river's delta, and most of it sits only about a metre above sea level behind a ring of dikes. That single fact shapes {SERVICE} here more than anything else: the water table is high, the ground is soft delta soil, and drainage is never an afterthought. A {BUSINESS_TYPE} that plans for those conditions delivers lasting results; one that ignores them learns expensive lessons on someone else's {PROPERTY_TYPE}.

Each neighbourhood presents its own version of the challenge. Steveston, once the salmon-canning capital of the BC coast and still home to the Gulf of Georgia Cannery National Historic Site and a working fishing harbour, mixes heritage buildings and boardwalk-facing commercial frontage with newer family homes. Brighouse and the wider City Centre corridor along No. 3 Road have transformed since the Canada Line opened in 2009 — condo towers near the Richmond Olympic Oval, the Aberdeen Centre and the Golden Village dining district, and strata buildings whose councils expect documented, professional work. West in Seafair and Terra Nova you find postwar ranchers on generous lots, many mid-renovation or being replaced outright. East toward Hamilton, the city turns agricultural, with cranberry and blueberry farms on protected farmland and rural properties that need providers willing to travel past No. 6 Road.

Then there is the weather. Metro Vancouver's November-to-March rain hits Richmond's flat, low-lying ground differently than it hits the North Shore slopes: water doesn't run off here, it lingers. Moss, algae, saturated soil and slow drainage are the local signature, and {COMPANY_NAME} builds every Richmond {SERVICE} plan around them — {SERVICE_DESCRIPTION}.

The economy raises the bar too. Vancouver International Airport anchors thousands of jobs on Sea Island, and the surrounding logistics, tech and hospitality businesses keep Richmond property owners busy and short on patience for no-shows. With {YEARS_IN_BUSINESS} years of experience and a {RATING} rating, {COMPANY_NAME} offers {SERVICE_LIST} with a {RESPONSE_TIME} response and a {ESTIMATE_TYPE} for any Richmond property — from a Steveston heritage storefront to a Terra Nova family home. Call {PHONE} and talk to a team that knows exactly what kind of ground this city stands on.
Local_Facts:
- Population: 209,937 (2021 Census, Statistics Canada)
- Occupies Lulu Island and Sea Island in the Fraser River delta, about 13 km south of Vancouver
- Home to Vancouver International Airport (YVR), one of Canada's busiest airports
- Steveston village was the historic centre of BC's salmon canning; the Gulf of Georgia Cannery is a National Historic Site
- Most of the city sits roughly one metre above sea level, protected by a dike network
- The Richmond Olympic Oval hosted long-track speed skating at the 2010 Winter Olympics
- The Canada Line (opened 2009) links Richmond City Centre to YVR and downtown Vancouver

FAQ_2_Question: Does Richmond being below the dikes on the Fraser River delta affect {SERVICE} work?
FAQ_2_Answer: Yes, and it's the main thing that separates Richmond from the rest of Metro Vancouver.
